opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I swallowed some chicken and it feels stuck right behind my breastbone; I can't even swallow my own spit without pain.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A piece of chicken got stuck behind my breastbone about two hours ago, I can't swallow my own saliva, and it hurts — I don't know what to do.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago, right in the middle of eating chicken
symptom_narrative.character: a stuck, pressured feeling behind my breastbone
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: distressing — I can't swallow spit and it hurts to try
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right behind my breastbone, in the middle of my chest
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing, I keep having to spit saliva out
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to swallow anything, even saliva
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: pressure behind the breastbone, bringing up thick saliva, and I'm really anxious
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: over the last six months food has been catching and I've had trouble swallowing dry solids, but it's never got fully stuck like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't eat or drink and I'm pacing and spitting into a bowl

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I'm on my own tonight and worried about choking or being sick while I'm asleep

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: likely elevated by anxiety and discomfort
